news.title: Key facts about Native Hawaiians and Pacific Islanders in the U.S.
news.summary_en: About 1.7 million people in the U.S. are Native Hawaiian or Pacific Islander (NHPI), tracing their roots to Hawaii, Guam, Samoa or other Pacific Islands in Oceania.

news.title: 5 facts about U.S. children living in blended families
news.summary_en: 17% of U.S. children under 18 live in a blended family most or all of the time – one that includes a stepparent, stepsiblings or half siblings.

news.title: What types of news do Americans seek out or happen to come across?
news.summary_en: About half of U.S. adults (49%) say they mostly get news because they happen to come across it, up from 39% in 2019.
